title: Fix Concatenating Non-Empty Strings with Null

statement.markdown: Given a list of strings, concatenate all non-empty strings in the list into a single string. If the input list contains nulls, they should be ignored.
statement.inputFormat: A list of strings where some elements may be null.
statement.outputFormat: A single concatenated string with all non-null elements from the input list.

hints.text: Use a conditional statement to filter out nulls from the list of strings.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: Iterate through the list of strings and keep track of non-null strings.
editorial.bruteForce: Use a for loop and conditional statements to check for nulls and concatenate non-null strings.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a StringBuilder or string concatenation operator to build the final string.
